## [00:00 - 03:00]

cool things about Google Analytics is that you can create segments and you can look at your traffic and your data by those segments as well. So here I'm just using this report as an example. I'm in source medium under all traffic and you can see the source and medium that's being sent to this Google Analytics account. Now if I wanted to I could segment out a different group of people and actually give you an idea of how Facebook is able to break down some of the data points to something that's a bit more meaningful for you. So for example, I've created this one here which is and I'm just going to click edit so you can see the makeup of it. This one will filter out the traffic, sorry the data below and only show traffic where these parameters match. Now bear in mind there are a lot of ways that you can slice and dice your data like this is super super advanced in what it can do but just for the simplicity here I'm going to show you how for example using our full funnel system I can limit the data so it just shows me people that are in my warm audience. So the way I have my full funnel is cold, warm, hot and existing customers. So what I'm saying here is I want my medium to be Facebook, sorry my source to be Facebook, my medium to be ads. This depends on how you've set up your data for your reporting. So going back to here in most cases this is what we use UTM medium but for this particular Google Analytics account it's been set up with UTM underscore medium equals ads and so because of that we are using ads here and then I'm using campaign warm. So what I'm doing here is saying if the campaign contains the word warm then pull out that data. Now it means that your campaigns have to be set up with the right data and the reporting to be able to pull it out in this way but let's just say you have done that. Now there's two things to bear in mind here, filtering users and filtering sessions are two very separate but similar things. Now when you filter by sessions that means that what Google Analytics is doing is looking at sessions as in what we might call visits. So sessions are, you know, someone's come to your website and spent a certain amount of time or done a certain amount of activities where the UTM campaign had warm, medium, source equals all of that whereas at the user level this is looking at, so in fact this is probably the best way to illustrate it. One user can have many sessions but you can't have one session with more than one user. So one physical person has to be on the other side. So you can see for example, you know, most people, most people, so users of people have had more than one session. So that's

## [03:00 - 05:14]

the way to kind of see this. This one says I want to see the individual people that have fallen into this bucket. This one says it's more about the session. So in Facebook or in AdLand it's the equivalent of reach and frequency. So reach is essentially users, frequency is session. So that's the way to kind of see this. Now I usually filter it by users because I want to see that level of data but you can do sessions. Give it a name, so I've called it fact for short and then Facebook warm traffic. Then when you hit save and we'll wait for this to load then the data below will be filtered. So the source and medium should be Facebook ads because that's what I've kind of asked for and then it will just show data for that. Now that alone isn't the most helpful. What's really helpful is when you start to look at different types of segments. So for example, you know, this tells me that this is my warm traffic. I could add cold, I could add hot, I could add lots of different, you can have more than one segment in play and then you can see the traffic, you can see the revenue and other metrics which are exposed. So for example, if I have a look at revenue right now, then it will show me a graph of revenue. Now on this one we've only just set this up late October says there wasn't really much tracking there but you can look at the revenue and you can see what came through warm you can then do the same and create a segment for cold and you can compare revenue day by day as a trend. The amount of stuff you can do is as I've said multiple times in this training is just this alone could be a huge area of learning for people. But what I want you to take away from this is when you're doing your analysis and your decision making and you've got your data set up in the right way, then really this is Pandora's box the amount of ways that you can cut and slice the data looking at you know e-commerce conversion, I could go into overview and start looking at how my warm traffic is performing, I can look at it by landing page and say you know how are they performing there, what's the engagement rate like etc. So you can start to make better decisions from your data using segments.
